from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a fluent, single sentence.
    Handles the specific predicates for MeanOfTransportation by using
    natural‑language templates.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a callable that returns a clause string.
    # The callable receives (subject, object) and returns a phrase.
    def _default(subject, obj):
        return f"{subject} has {obj}"

    templates = {
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
        "bodyStyle": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a {o.lower()} body style",
        "engine": lambda s, o: f"{s} is powered by {o}",
        "manufacturer": lambda s, o: f"{s} was manufactured by {o}",
        "relatedMeanOfTransportation": lambda s, o: f"{s} is related to {o}",
        "transmission": lambda s, o: f"{s} features a {o.lower()} transmission",
        "wheelbase": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a wheelbase of {o}",
        "builder": lambda s, o: f"{s} was built by {o}",
        "completionDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was completed on {o}",
        "length": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} long",
        "powerType": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} power",
        "shipClass": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the {o.lower()} class",
        "shipDisplacement": lambda s, o: f"{s} displaces {o}",
        "shipLaunch": lambda s, o: f"{s} was launched on {o}",
        "shipOrdered": lambda s, o: f"{s} was ordered on {o}",
        "shipPower": lambda s, o: f"{s} is equipped with {o}",
        "topSpeed": lambda s, o: f"{s} can reach a top speed of {o} knots",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "christeningDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was christened on {o}",
        "maidenVoyage": lambda s, o: f"{s} made its maiden voyage on {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "shipBeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a beam of {o} metres",
        "shipInService": lambda s, o: f"{s} entered service on {o}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{s} is currently {o.lower()}",
        "activeYearsStartDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} started active years on {o}",
        "shipLaidDown": lambda s, o: f"{s} was laid down on {o}",
        "buildDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was built between {o}",
        "cylinderCount": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} cylinders",
        "totalProduction": lambda s, o: f"{s} produced {o} units",
        "countryOrigin": lambda s, o: f"{s} originated from {o}",
        "diameter": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a diameter of {o}",
        "failedLaunches": lambda s, o: f"{s} suffered {o} failed launch(es)",
        "rocketStages": lambda s, o: f"{s} consists of {o} stages",
        "totalLaunches": lambda s, o: f"{s} performed {o} launch(es)",
        "assembly": lambda s, o: f"{s} was assembled in {o}",
        "class": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the {o.lower()} class",
        "designer": lambda s, o: f"{s} was designed by {o}",
        "modelYears": lambda s, o: f"{s} was produced in {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a country called {o}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {o}",
        "designCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} was designed by {o}",
        "productionStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started production in {o}",
        "width": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} metres wide",
        "layout": lambda s, o: f"{s} features a {o.lower()} layout",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a subsidiary of {o}",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "product": lambda s, o: f"{s} produces {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "successor": lambda s, o: f"{s} succeeded by {o}",
        "fate": lambda s, o: f"{s} met its fate as {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"{s} had key person {o}",
        "subsidiary": lambda s, o: f"{s} owned subsidiary {o}",
        "comparable": lambda s, o: f"{s} is comparable to {o}",
        "finalFlight": lambda s, o: f"{s} had its final flight on {o}",
        "function": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves as {o.lower()}",
        "launchSite": lambda s, o: f"{s} launched from {o}",
        "maidenFlight": lambda s, o: f"{s} first flew on {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"{s} has capital {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The people are called {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by {o}",
        "partialFailures": lambda s, o: f"{s} experienced {o} partial failure(s)",
        "site": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located at {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"{s} has headquarters in {o}",
        "associatedRocket": lambda s, o: f"{s} is associated with {o}",
        "saint": lambda s, o: f"The patron saint of {s} is {o}",
        "employer": lambda s, o: f"{s} works for {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"The {o} are an ethnic group in {s}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} holds the title {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is \"{o}\"",
        "productionEndYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} ceased production in {o}",
        "division": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a division of {o}",
        "shipDraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a draft of {o}",
        "modelStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started model year in {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o} km²",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "extinctionDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} became extinct on {o}",
    }

    # Build clauses using the template map
    clauses = []
    prev_subject = None
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, _default)
        # Use pronoun "It" for repeated subjects to avoid redundancy
        subject = t.subject
        if prev_subject is not None and t.subject == prev_subject:
            subject = "It"
        clause = tmpl(subject, t.object)
        # If we used the pronoun, make the clause start with a lower‑case verb
        if subject == "It":
            clause = clause[0].lower() + clause[1:]
        clauses.append(clause)
        prev_subject = t.subject

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        # Oxford comma style
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
